event: Construction of the Great Pyramid of Giza is completed.
The first recorded Olympic Games are held in ancient Greece.
Julius Caesar is assassinated on the Ides of March.
Mount Vesuvius erupts, destroying Pompeii and Herculaneum.
Ts'ai Lun invents the modern papermaking process in China.
Vikings raid the monastery at Lindisfarne, marking the start of the Viking Age.
William the Conqueror invades England and wins the Battle of Hastings.
King John of England signs the Magna Carta at Runnymede.
The Black Death plague arrives in Europe via trading ships.
Johannes Gutenberg invents the movable-type printing press.
Christopher Columbus makes landfall in the Americas.
Martin Luther posts his 95 Theses, sparking the Protestant Reformation.
Nicolaus Copernicus publishes his heliocentric theory of the solar system.
The Gregorian Calendar is introduced by Pope Gregory XIII.
Jamestown, Virginia is founded as the first permanent English settlement in North America.
Galileo Galilei discovers the four largest moons of Jupiter.
The Great Fire of London destroys most of the medieval city.
Sir Isaac Newton publishes the 'Principia Mathematica', detailing the laws of motion.
Benjamin Franklin famously conducts his kite experiment to prove lightning is electricity.
The United States Declaration of Independence is adopted.
The storming of the Bastille triggers the start of the French Revolution.
Eli Whitney invents the cotton gin, revolutionizing the textile industry.
Edward Jenner develops the world's first successful vaccine, targeting smallpox.
The United States purchases the Louisiana Territory from France.
Napoleon Bonaparte crowns himself Emperor of the French.
Napoleon is definitively defeated at the Battle of Waterloo.
Joseph Nicéphore Niépce takes the world's first permanent photograph.
Queen Victoria ascends to the British throne, beginning the Victorian Era.
Samuel Morse sends the first official telegraph message: 'What hath God wrought?'.
Gold is discovered at Sutter's Mill, sparking the California Gold Rush.
Charles Darwin publishes 'On the Origin of Species'.
The American Civil War begins with the bombardment of Fort Sumter.
President Abraham Lincoln delivers the Gettysburg Address.
The Thirteenth Amendment is ratified, officially abolishing slavery in the US.
The First Transcontinental Railroad is completed in the United States at Promontory Summit.
Alexander Graham Bell receives the patent for the telephone.
Thomas Edison successfully tests his long-lasting incandescent light bulb.
Karl Benz patents the Benz Patent-Motorwagen, widely regarded as the first modern automobile.
The Statue of Liberty is officially dedicated in New York Harbor.
The Eiffel Tower officially opens in Paris for the World's Fair.
New Zealand becomes the first self-governing country to grant women the right to vote.
The first modern Olympic Games open in Athens, Greece.
The Wright brothers achieve the first sustained, controlled airplane flight.
Ford Motor Company introduces the Model T automobile.
The RMS Titanic strikes an iceberg and sinks on its maiden voyage.
The assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand triggers World War I.
The Bolshevik Revolution overthrows the Russian monarchy.
The 19th Amendment is ratified, granting American women the right to vote.
Howard Carter discovers the intact tomb of King Tutankhamun.
Charles Lindbergh completes the first solo, nonstop transatlantic flight.
Alexander Fleming discovers penicillin, opening the era of antibiotics.
The Wall Street stock market crash sparks the Great Depression.
The German airship Hindenburg catches fire and destroys itself while landing in New Jersey.
Germany invades Poland, triggering the outbreak of World War II.
Japan attacks Pearl Harbor, drawing the United States into World War II.
Allied forces land on the beaches of Normandy on D-Day.
The United Nations is officially established to maintain international peace.
Jackie Robinson breaks the Major League Baseball color barrier with the Dodgers.
James Watson and Francis Crick deduce the double-helix structure of DNA.
The US Supreme Court rules segregation unconstitutional in Brown v. Board of Education.
The Soviet Union launches Sputnik 1, the first artificial satellite, starting the Space Race.
Yuri Gagarin becomes the first human to travel into outer space.
Martin Luther King Jr. delivers his 'I Have a Dream' speech during the March on Washington.
The Beatles appear on The Ed Sullivan Show, triggering 'Beatlemania' in the US.
Apollo 11 lands on the Moon; Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin walk on the surface.
The Atari console game Pong is released, launching the coin-operated arcade industry.
The US military officially ends its direct combat involvement in the Vietnam War.
Microsoft is founded by Bill Gates and Paul Allen.
The original 'Star Wars' movie premieres in theaters.
The 'Miracle on Ice' occurs as the US Olympic hockey team defeats the Soviet Union.
IBM introduces its first Personal Computer (PC), the Model 5150.
The ARPANET officially switches to TCP/IP protocol, creating the technical foundation of the Internet.
The Live Aid benefit concerts take place simultaneously in London and Philadelphia.
The Chernobyl nuclear power plant suffers a catastrophic reactor explosion.
The Berlin Wall is opened, leading to the reunification of East and West Germany.
Tim Berners-Lee writes the first web server and browser, launching the World Wide Web.
The Soviet Union officially dissolves, ending the Cold War.
The European Union is formally established by the signing of the Maastricht Treaty.
Nelson Mandela is inaugurated as the first democratically elected president of South Africa.
Dolly the Sheep, the first mammal cloned from an adult somatic cell, is born.
The first Harry Potter book, 'Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone', is published.
Google is officially incorporated as a privately held company by Larry Page and Sergey Brin.
The International Space Station welcomes its first long-term resident crew.
The September 11 terrorist attacks occur on the World Trade Center and Pentagon.
Facebook is launched by Mark Zuckerberg out of his Harvard dorm room.
The video-sharing website YouTube is founded by three former PayPal employees.
Apple CEO Steve Jobs introduces the first-generation iPhone.
Barack Obama is elected as the first African American President of the United States.
NASA officially retires its Space Shuttle program after the final flight of Atlantis.
Scientists at CERN announce the discovery of a particle consistent with the Higgs Boson.
The Paris Agreement on climate change is adopted by nearly 200 nations.
The Chicago Cubs win the World Series for the first time in 108 years.
The Event Horizon Telescope captures the first direct image of a black hole.
The World Health Organization officially declares COVID-19 a global pandemic.
The James Webb Space Telescope is successfully launched into orbit.
Russia launches a full-scale military invasion of Ukraine.
India's Chandrayaan-3 successfully lands a rover near the lunar south pole.
The total solar eclipse tracks across North America from Mexico to Canada.
NASA launches the Artemis II mission, sending a crewed spacecraft around the Moon.
